segments.text: This would tie is 5.18.180. Once the exalted one with a great following of
monks walked a tour among the cosalans and as the exalted one went along the
highway he saw a place where grew a big grove of salt trees and seeing it there
he moved down from the road and went towards it and made his way his way
among the trees and at a certain place he smiled. Now the venerable Ananda
thought what cause now what reason moved the exalted one to show a smile not
for nothing do the Targata smile and the venerable Ananda asked him concerning
the matter then said the exalted one at this place Ananda in olden times there
was a rich and flourishing city thronged with many people and by the city
Ananda there dwelt the exalted one Kasapa Arahan fully enlightened and
Gavesin was a lay disciple of the exalted one Kasapa but he kept not the
moral precepts now because of Gavesin there were about 500 who testified and
were stirred to discipleship but they kept not the moral precepts thought he
I have greatly served these 500 lay disciples being the first to move and
disturb myself yet I keep not the moral precepts nor do these 500 others this is
a leveling of levels leaving no width of a more come now I'm for something more
I stopped here for a while this Gavesin he had 500 friends and he was the first
to become a disciple of the Buddha Kasapa which was the Buddha previous to
our Sakya Muni Buddha and when he became a disciple of the Kasapa Buddha his
500 friends also became disciples of Kasapa Buddha but all of them did not
keep the precepts so he said this is a leveling of levels leaving no width of a
more that means this is the lowest lowest and at the stage they are at
so he said I'm for something more he should aim for something higher now the
Suta continues so Gavesin went up to the 500 and said no good sirs that from
today I keep the moral precepts then Ananda those 500 thought to themselves
verily master Gavesin has greatly served us being first to move and disturb
himself and the same master Gavesin will now keep the moral precepts why
then not we too then when those 500 lay disciples to Gavesin and told him that
they too henceforth would keep the moral precepts again thought Gavesin this
also is a leveling of levels leaving no width of a more I'm for something more
and he went and told them saying no sirs from today I follow the celibate's
life the life remote giving up sex life common among men then thought those
others why not we too and did likewise again thought Gavesin this is also a
leveling of levels leaving no width of a more I'm for something more and went and
told them saying no sirs from today I am a one mealer and abstain from night
eating giving up eating at wrong times then thought they why not we too and did
likewise now the lay disciple Gavesin considered thus I have greatly served
these 500 lay disciples being the first to move and disturb myself I have kept
the moral precepts and now they too have done this I have followed the live
remote and now they too have done this I have become a one mealer and now they
too have done this this is indeed a leveling of levels leaving no width of a
more come now I'm for something more and Ananda the lay disciple Gavesin went to
the exalted one Kasapa Arahan Sammasambuddha and having come said to him
Lord grant that I may go forth beside the exalted one grant me full ordination
and Gavesin the lay disciple obtain permission to go forth beside Kasapa
the exalted one Arahan Sammasambuddha and he obtained full ordination now
Ananda not long after his ordination Gavesin the monk living alone withdrawn
diligent ardent resolved entered into an abode in that unsurpassed goal of the
holy life here in this very life realizing it by his own knowledge that
goal for the good of which Klansman's sons rightly go forth from the home to
the homeless life and he declared this destroyed his birth lived is the holy
life done is the task there is no more of this to come there after Ananda the
man Gavesin was numbered among the arahans then thought those 500 lay
disciples master Gavesin has done us a great service he was the first to move
and disturb himself and now he has had his hair and beard shaved off don the
yellow rope and goes forth from the home to the homeless life why then not me too
and those 500 lay disciples and Ananda when and beg the exalted one Kasapa
saying Grand Lord that we may go forth beside the exalted one grant us full
ordination and those 500 obtain permission then thought the monk Gavesin
I verily can obtain this unsurpassed bliss of liberation at will easily
and without difficulty would that those 500 monks could obtain this bliss in
like manner Ananda those 500 monks a boat alone withdrawn zealous ardent
resolved and not long after entered and dwelt in that unsurpassed goal of the
holy life here in this very life realizing it by their own knowledge that
for which clansmen sons rightly go forth and declared destroyed his birth lived
is the holy life done is the task there is no more of this to come thus verily
Ananda these 500 monks with Gavesin at their head in striving from higher things
to higher from strength to strength came to realize the liberation above which
there is no higher where for ananda train yourselves in this way from higher
to higher from strength to strength we will strive and will come to realize
the liberation above which there is no higher verily thus ananda train
yourselves as the end of the sutta this is quite an inspiring sutta and it
shows how good friends help each other in life pulling each other up along the
way and this Gavesin he was a very good friend to these 500 people he was the
first to blaze the trail show them the way he was the first to become a
disciple then kept the precepts then kept the eight precepts and finally
renounced and became an arahan and they followed him and they also walk the
good way so in this shows when we have a good friend a friend who knows the
dhamma and he can inspire us and that's very good then we because we are
generally quite influenced by our friends if you join with friends who do
are not interested in the dhamma who walk the evil way the foolish way and we
will also end up like then but when we join with good friends then they help
us and we and we help each other along the path this one in one sutta ananda
told the Buddha he said he thinks that half of the holy life have to do with
good friends and the Buddha said no ananda the whole of the holy life has to
do with good friends so good friends in the spiritual path is very important we
inspire each other and help each other in the last part you note that the 500
monks and also Gavesin they abode alone withdrawn zealous ardent resolve and not
long after entered and dwelt in that answer pass goal of the holy life so you
see generally these are hands they to obtain the highest state and they lived
alone when you live alone there's nobody to distract you to talk to then you
spend more time in meditation undisturbed and then the progress would be
much faster sometimes if you don't stay alone we stay in company here like in a
monastery then it is good to practice aloofness that means we don't talk too
much now even though we are in the company of other people in a monastery
we try to limit our talk and practice more the next two times five point
nineteen point one eight one the Buddha said miles these five are forest gone
what five one is forest gone out of folly and blindness one out of evil
desires and longings one foolish and mind tossed one at the thought it is
praised by the buddhas and their disciples and one is forest gone just
because his ones are little just for contentment just for austerity just for
seclusion just because it is the very thing barely monks of these five who
have gone to the forest he who has gone just because his ones are little for
contentment for austerity for seclusion just because it is the very thing he of
the five is top most best foremost highest elec monks just as from the power
comes milk from milk cream from cream butter from butter ghee from ghee the
skim of ghee they reckon top most even so months of these five for us gone he
who has gone just because his ones are little for contentment for austerity
for seclusion and just because it is the very thing he of the five is top most
best foremost highest elec so this uta is talking about people who go and live
alone in the forest for various reasons but the best one is the last one
and because he wants to cultivate himself his ones are little he is contented
he wants to practice austerity's and seclusion and that is the best reason
the next uta is 5.20.191 the Buddha said monks these five ancient brahmin
things are now seen in dogs but not in brahmin's what five monks in former
times brahmin's approach only a brahmani never a non brahmani now they go to
brahmani and non brahmani alike monks today dogs go to dogs only never to
other creatures this month is the first ancient brahmin thing now seen in dogs
but not in brahmin's monks in former times brahmin's approach a brahmani or
brahmini only in season never at other at other times now they go to a brahmani
both in and out of season monks today dogs go to dogs only in season never at
other times this month is the second ancient brahmin thing now seen in dogs
not in brahmin's monks in former times brahmin's neither bought nor sold a
brahmani but consorting just where affection was mutual foster concord now
they do any of these things
monks today dogs neither buy nor sell dogs but consort just where affection is
mutual and foster concord this month is the third ancient brahmin thing now seen
in dogs not in brahmin's monks in former times brahmin's ordered neither
treasure grain silver nor gold now they do these things monks today dogs hot
neither treasure grain silver nor gold this month is the fourth ancient brahmin
thing now seen in dogs not in brahmin's monks in former times brahmin sought food
for the evening meal in the evening for the morning meal in the morning now
after cramming their bellies to the uttermost they take away the remainder
monks today dogs seek food for the evening meal in the evening for the
morning meal in the morning this month is the fifth ancient brahmin thing now
seen in dogs not in brahmin's barely monks these are the five ancient
brahmin things as the end of the sutta this is quite an unusual sutta where
the Buddha is investigating the brahmin's is comparing them to dogs and
saying that dogs are even better than these brahmin's these brahmin's
originally were supposed to be priests, ascetics and they were supposed to after
their certain years of training they're supposed to be like renunciant where
they bake for their food and all that and if they don't want to continue their
that life then if they get married they only get married to a brahmani that
means one of the brahmin cast also a female of the brahmin cast and they
only like sleep only when the woman is in season and just to get a child not for
enjoyment but now this Buddha is saying comparing them to dogs the first one that
in ancient times brahmin's only approach a brahmani now they approach a
brahmani and a non brahmani other cast also whereas dogs only go to dogs to
procreate and they still do and the second one is that brahmin only
approach a brahmani during her season not at other times just as but but now
they they they do it at any time the one whereas dogs still keep keep the ancient
tradition of going to the beach only in season not at other times and then brahmin's
formerly neither bought nor sold a brahmani but sort of got married and
just where the affection was mutual and nowadays they buy and sell brahmani's
whereas dogs still don't do that and then in ancient times brahmin's never
hoarded their their things like never hoarded treasure grains silver or gold
and now they hoard these things whereas dogs still don't do that and the last
one is brahmin's formerly used to seek for their food in the evening if they
need food in the morning they'll go and look for it in the morning if they need
it in the evening they look for it in the evening they never store their food
whereas now they eat as much as they can and then take away the remainder and
store it up whereas dogs are so still don't store their food so the Buddha is
saying that dogs are even better than brahmin's and the reason you can see if
you study more the sutras is that the brahmin's were very critical of the
Buddha they taught themselves as the superior caste and they look down on the
Buddha and and said that they were born from the mouth of Brahma their God they
were born from the mouth of Brahma whereas all other castes the other three
castes were born from the feet of Brahma and of course the other castes didn't
accept it because like the Buddha he belonged to the warrior caste the
kathya or kathya caste and that was the nobles and of course the nobles
considered themselves higher than the brahmin's but the brahmin's considered
themselves higher and so because they were arch enemies sort of that's why
the Buddha really castigated them in this sutra and this sutra also shows that
the Buddha also has his own peculiar habits his behavior and seems to show
if you study the sutras that the Buddha still has some of these human qualities
that carried over from previous life whereas this contrary to what the
commentary tries to say and the commentary tries to say that the Buddha is so
perfect that he has no faults whatsoever compared to the arahants arahants are
supposed to still have faults and peculiarities in their behavior and this
sutra is one of those that shows that the Buddha still has holes now the next
sutra is 5.20.192 now Brahmin donor visited the exalted one and greeted
him and after exchanging the customary words of greeting sat down at one side
so seated Brahmin donor said to the exalted one I have heard it said
master Gotama that master Gotama does not salute aged venerable brahmin's well
stricken in years long on life's road grown old nor rise up for them nor
offer them a seat master Gotama it is just so master Gotama does none of these
things to aged venerable brahmin's this is not right master Gotama and the Buddha
said do you not profess to be a Brahmin donor and donor said if of anyone master
Gotama in speaking rightly it should be said the Brahmin is well born on both
sides pure in descent as far back as seven generations both of mother and
father unchallenged and without reproach in point of birth
studious carrying the mantras in mind a past master in the three Vedas with the
indices and ritual in phonology too and fifthly in the legends an expert in verse
and grammar skilled in reading the marks of a great man in speculation on the
universe to be sure of me master Gotama in speaking rightly that thing should be
said for I master Gotama I'm so born I'm so skilled I'll just stop here for a
while so here this Brahmin is criticizing the Buddha for not paying
respect to old aged Brahmins and then instead of answering him directly the
Buddha asked him a question are you Brahmin and then he said that he spoke
about the three marks of a Brahmin the first one is that a Brahmin a pure Brahmin
is pure on the for seven generations he's born of pure blood Brahmins on the
mother and father side for seven generations that's the first mark of a
Brahmin the second one is that he's learned in the Vedas in the mantras
etc the third one is he has worldly knowledge that means he's killed in the
marks of a great man in speculation about the universe etc so he said he has all
these three marks of a Brahmin then the Buddha said donor those Brahmin sages of
all mantra makers mantra seers whose ancient collection of mantra verses
hymns and sayings Brahmins now ever hymn ever say ever word the word ever have
the saying said namely Ataka Vamaka Vamadeva Vesamita Yamadagi Angirasa
Bharadvaja Vaseta Kasapa and Baghu they these five Brahmins have declared the
Brahma like Deva like the bounded the breaker of bounds and fifthly the
Brahmin outcasts which of them donor are you and donor said we know not of these
five Brahmins master Gotama yet we know that we are Brahmins it will well for me
if master Gotama would teach me Dhamma so that I may know of these five I'll
stop here for a while here the Buddha is saying that those Brahmin sages of all
and he named them Ataka Vamaka Vamadeva Vesamita etc see the Buddha he knows
the he has this ability to see the path so because of his ability his psychic
power he could name all these Brahmin sages of all and he says these are
mantra makers mantra is a kind of words which have no meaning they are supposed
to be derived from the spiritual world but they have a certain power behind them
so these Brahmins they keep these mantras very secret and they will never
divulge these secrets to other castes they only keep it among their castes and
they use this mantras quite often and sometimes when they view these mantras
they have certain power that's why other castes sometimes are afraid of them so
the Buddha is saying that these Brahmin sages of all they have declared there
are five types of Brahmins the Brahma like the Deva like the bounded the
breaker of bounds and the Brahmin outcasts and he asked Dona which
category do you fall under and Dona he never heard of these five Brahmins so
the here he's asking the Buddha to explain these five classes of Brahmins
to him and the Buddha said then listen Brahmin give heed and I will speak yes
sir replied he and the exalted one said and how Dona becomes a Brahmin Brahma
like take the case Dona of a Brahmin who is well born on both sides pure in
descent as far back as seven generations both of mother and father
unchallenged and without reproach in point of birth he for eight and forty
years leech the Brahma life of virginity applying himself to the mantras
then completing that course he seeks the teacher's fee for teaching according to
Dhamma not done non-dhamma and what there is Dhamma Dona never as plowman nor
trader nor cowherd nor bowman nor Rajasman nor by any craft to get his
living but solely by going about for arms despising not the beggars bowl and he
hands over the teacher's fee for teaching and has his hair and beard shaped
of dawns the yellow rope and goes forth from the home to the homeless life and
thus gone forth he abides in mind pervading with goodwill one quarter of
the world so a second a third and a fourth quarter then above below across and
everywhere the whole wide world he pervades with thoughts of goodwill far
reaching expensive measureless without hatred or ill will he abides in mind
pervading with compassion with joy with equanimity one quarter of the world so a
second a third and a fourth quarter then above below across everywhere the whole
wide world he pervades with thoughts of compassion joy and equanimity far
reaching expensive measureless without hatred or ill will and having made these
four Brahma abidings become on the breaking up of the body after death he
arises in the well-faring Brahma world thus Dona a Brahmin becomes Brahma like
and how Dona becomes a Brahmin deva like take the case Dona of a Brahmin of
similar birth and conduct he does not get a living by plowing and so forth but
by going about for arms he hands over the teachers fee for teaching and seeks
a wife according to Dhamma not non-Dhamma and what there is Dhamma not with
one board or soul but only with a Brahmani on whom water has been poured
and he goes only to a Brahmani not to the daughter of a noble or the merchant
class or the laborer caste nor to the daughter of an outcast hunter a bamboo
worker a card maker or aboriginal nor go see to a woman with child nor to one
giving suck nor to one not in her season and why Dona goes not a Brahmin to one
with child if he go the boy or girl will surely be foully born therefore he goes
not and why go see not to one giving suck if he go the boy or girl will surely
be an unclean suckling therefore he goes not and where for go see not to one
not in her season if donor a Brahmin go to one in her season never for him does
the Brahmani become a means for lust for sport for pleasure the Brahmani is
for the Brahmin just a means to be gate of spring Buddha continued and when it
went long he has begotten a child he has his hair and beard shaved off and goes
forth and being thus gone forth aloof from sensual desires he enters and abides
in the first the second the third and the fourth Janas and having made these four
Janas become on the breaking up of the body after death he arises in the
well-faring heaven world thus donor a Brahmin becomes deva like and how donor
becomes a Brahmin bounded take the case donor of a Brahmin of similar
birth and conduct who wets in like manner and when in wedlock he has begotten
a child the fondness for children obsesses him and he settles on the family
estate and does not go forth on the home to the homeless life in the bounds of
the Brahmins of old he stays and transgresses and transgresses them not
and it is said within bounds he keeps and transgresses not and therefore the
Brahmin is called bounded thus donor the Brahmin becomes bounded and how
donor becomes a Brahmin a breaker of bounds take the case donor of a Brahmin
of similar birth and conduct he hands over the teacher's fee and seeks a wife
either according to dhamma or non-dhamma one bought or sold or a Brahmani on
whom the water pouring ceremony has been performed he goes to a Brahmani or to
the daughter of a noble or of a merchant caste man or of a laborer caste to the
daughter of an outcast or a hunter or a bamboo worker or a card maker or an
aboriginal he goes to a woman with child to one giving suck to one in her season
to one not in her season and for him the Brahmani becomes just a means for lust
for sport and for pleasure or to begin offspring and he keeps not within the
ancient Brahmin bounds but transgresses them and it is said he keeps not within
bounds but transgresses and therefore he is called a breaker of bounds thus donor
the Brahmin becomes a breaker of bounds and how donor becomes a Brahmin a Brahmin
outcast take the case donor of a Brahmin of similar birth he for 8 and 40
years reached the Brahma life of virginity applying himself to the
mantras then completing that course he seeks the teacher's fee for teaching he
gets his living according to dhamma or non-dhamma as a plough man a trader a
cowherd a bowman a rajas man all by some craft or despising not the beggars bowl
just by going about for arms on handing back the teacher's fee he seeks a wife
according to dhamma or non-dhamma one board or soul or a Brahmani on whom
water has been poured he goes to a Brahmani or any other woman one with
child giving suck and so forth and she is for him a means for lust or to begin
offspring he leads a life doing all these things then the Brahmin say thus of
him how is it that an honorable Brahmin leads this sort of life and to this he
replies just as fire burns clean things or unclean and not by that is the fire
defile even so good sirs if a Brahma if a Brahmin lead a life doing all these
things not by doing that is a Brahmin defile and it is said he leads a life
doing all these things and therefore he is called a Brahmin outcast thus donor
a Brahmin becomes a Brahmin outcast fairly donor those Brahmin sages of all
mantra makers mantra says whose ancient collection of mantra verses hymns and
sayings Brahmins now still him say word each word and have the saying said
namely ataka and the rest these five Brahmins declare have declared the
Brahma like the Deva like the bounded the breaker of bounds and fifthly the
Brahmin outcasts which of them donor are you and donor said if such there are
master gotama we at least do not fulfill the ways of the Brahmin outcast but it
is marvelous what you say a master gotama let master gotama take me as a
lay disciple gone to his refuge henceforth as life as long as life last
as the end of the sutta is one of the sutras and where you can see and the
Buddha this vast knowledge he can teach a Brahmin even the Brahmin tradition
that they don't know of and here he's describing the five types of Brahmins
now you notice all of them for the first 48 years of their life they lead a life
of virginity and then they learn the mantras and after that they start
teaching and then if they follow the tradition then they are supposed to
only beg for their food not to take up a trade and the first three types are
still acceptable to them that means the first one after 48 years of learning
then he renounces becomes a renunciation and practices the Brahma abidings
Brahma Viharas which brings him to rebirth in the Brahma world the second
one he seeks a wife but only a Brahmin on whom water has been put been poured
that means I suppose means that this has been given to him because the ceremony
of pouring water I think implies that giving so he doesn't go to any other
woman of any other caste only to a Brahmani and once he has a child with
this Brahmani then he shapes of his hair and goes forth and attains the Janas
so that after that he's reborn in the heaven world as a second class the third
one after 48 years of learning then he gets married also also to a Brahmin a
Brahmani and after that after he has a child instead of renouncing he is
attached to the child and he stays in the home he doesn't renounce still that
is acceptable but the last two are not acceptable because the the last two
they might marry a Brahmani or they might not marry a Brahmani once they
marry out of their caste that is not acceptable and also they are because of
the other conditions mentioned so this Sutta is interesting from the because it
gives us and insight into the life of a Brahmin during those times because
nowadays you hardly see this happen nowadays you don't see Brahmins who
are virgins for 48 years of their life learning all the mantras they have kind
of lost their tradition
